what_it_does: Implements the ForagerBar React component, a minimized-by-default top-center HUD that lets the user submit a corpus query against a selected projection space, renders returned hits as ForagerShape circles on the tldraw canvas, filters their visibility by live kind/session chips, indicates the address of a single selected circle via the app context, and clears only forager shapes.
observations: Uses tldraw hooks useEditor and useValue plus useApp().indicate to integrate with the existing canvas and locus machinery.
Fetches the embeddable space list from api.cognitionInfo and surfaces a fallback to history/repo with an info note when the registry is empty or errors.
Search is explicit only: Enter key or button click triggers api.corpusQuery; no keystroke search.
Empty and error states are rendered loudly via EmptyState or a note div, never swallowed.
Chips are derived from the live ForagerShape props kind and session, sorted alphabetically, and toggling a chip hides non-matching shapes by setting opacity to 0 and deselecting them.
Clear removes only shapes whose type is forager and leaves graph nodes untouched.
The component starts minimized and the whole header strip toggles minimization.
data-ui-ref attributes are attached to several elements for external UI targeting.
standouts: Filter hides rather than deletes, and the comment specifies that ForagerShape.getGeometry collapses hit geometry at opacity 0 so hidden circles cannot be selected unseen.
Explicit submit with an anti-keystroke-search guard to avoid embedder load.
Honest empty/error rendering including backend-provided notes.
Single-forager selection feeds the existing indicate() locus rather than introducing a separate indication channel.
conventions: Follows React functional-component hooks patterns and tldraw reactive reads via useValue.
Uses the kit EmptyState component and .rhm/.hud card class anatomy shared with chat panels.
Imperatively updates shape opacity through editor.updateShapes inside the filter helper.
Uses any-typed API responses and an eslint-disable for the indicate effect dependency array.
concerns: applyFilters is called imperatively and reads the full current-page shape list; it is not wrapped in an editor transaction, which could produce intermediate store states under rapid toggles.
The clear action deselects and deletes forager shapes but does not explicitly cancel an in-flight search or reset the query.
Chip counts include hidden shapes because they are derived from all loaded forager shapes, not only currently visible ones.
The comment claims the bar is hidden below 699px, but no media-query logic appears in this file.
notes: A self-contained search lane that turns corpus hits into canvas primitives and whittles them via live facet chips without deleting anything.
questions: What is the exact response contract for api.corpusQuery and api.cognitionInfo?
How does placeForagerHits position hits and handle deduplication/pulsing?
Where is the <699px hiding rule implemented if not in this file?
What values can kind and session take, and are they validated anywhere?
How does indicate propagate the address to chats and the builder?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file implements the described search-to-circles flow, explicit submit, honest empty/error states, chip filtering, and forager-only clearing. The mobile breakpoint is mentioned in comments but not enforced here.
apparent_themes: Operator control through explicit actions and non-destructive filtering
Transparency via loud empty, error, and fallback states
Registry-is-truth: chips and spaces come from actual data, not hardcoded vocabularies
Coexistence: forager shapes live alongside graph shapes and are scoped separately
Reuse of existing canvas machinery rather than parallel systems
intention_signals: The file is trying to build a lightweight, trustworthy corpus-search surface that produces tangible canvas objects, lets the operator narrow results visually, and feeds those results into downstream builder context without imposing on the rest of the UI.
novelty: Dynamic facet chips generated from loaded shape metadata, a hide-via-opacity-plus-geometry-collapse filter that prevents hidden shapes from being selected, and an explicit-submit design paired with loud empty/error notes.
contribution: It contributes a complete search-to-shape interaction pattern: explicit corpus query submission, transparent result states, live metadata-driven filtering, non-destructive forager-only cleanup, and integration with existing selection and indication flows.
summary_for_embedding: ForagerBar is a minimized React HUD component that queries a corpus via an API, renders hits as tldraw ForagerShape circles, filters them by live kind/session chips, indicates selected addresses, and clears only forager shapes.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to give users a controlled, transparent search lane that turns corpus results into manipulable canvas primitives and narrows them without destructive edits.

symbols.does: Defines a local Note union type for status messages.
symbols.description: Note is a small type alias used for the component's note state, distinguishing error, empty, and info messages so the UI can choose the right presentation.

symbols.does: Renders the top-center corpus-search HUD and manages its query, space, results, chips, and filtering.
symbols.description: ForagerBar is the main React component. It holds state for minimization, query text, selected space, live status notes, busy state, and focused chip set. It fetches spaces, submits corpus queries, places hits as ForagerShape circles, derives kind/session chips from the live shape set, applies visibility filters, and wires a single selected forager to the app's indicate() function.

symbols.does: Sets opacity and deselects forager shapes based on the current kind/session chip focus set.
symbols.description: applyFilters reads all current forager shapes, splits the focused set into kind and session facets, computes visibility as union within each facet and intersection across facets, updates each shape's opacity through editor.updateShapes, and deselects any shapes that become hidden.

symbols.does: Adds or removes a chip key from the focused set and reapplies filters.
symbols.description: toggleChip copies the current focused set, toggles the given kind: or session: key, updates React state, and immediately calls applyFilters so the canvas reflects the new focus.

symbols.does: Submits the current query and space to the corpus API and places or reports the results.
symbols.description: search validates the query, calls api.corpusQuery with the selected space and k=16, renders loud error or empty notes, places hits on the canvas via placeForagerHits, reapplies any active chip filters, and reports the number of new and re-hit circles plus any backend note.
